_comment: SHARED golden for the console-text canonicalizer — a SECOND implementation of the same canonical form exists in each repo (runner: canonicalizeConsole in transientClass.ts; api: TraceSignalsDiff.Canonicalize in Infrastructure/TraceSignalsDiff.cs). Both MUST produce byte-identical `expected` for every `input`, or the flake budget (runner) and the error diff (api) would disagree about what counts as 'the same error' — the exact class of bug that made the transient classifier lie (#299). Enforced by canonicalizeGoldenParity.test.ts (runner) + TraceSignalsGoldenParity (api). `expected` was generated from the runner impl and asserted equal on both sides. To prove the gate can fail: change one regex in EITHER impl and this reds.

cases.name: hashed_chunk_deploy_stability
cases.why: a per-deploy chunk hash (>=12 chars) collapses to '*' so the SAME ChunkLoadError keys identically across deploys

cases.name: hashed_chunk_different_deploy
cases.why: a DIFFERENT hash for the same chunk must yield the SAME canonical key as above (deploy stability)

cases.name: short_hash_below_threshold_not_collapsed
cases.why: documents the LONG_TOKEN {12,} threshold — a 6-char id is left intact; both impls agree (no divergence)

cases.name: iso_timestamp_stripped
cases.why: an ISO-8601 timestamp is removed so two errors that differ only by when they logged key the same

cases.name: query_string_stripped
cases.why: a ?query (cache-buster / token / session id) is removed to the next whitespace/quote/paren

cases.name: long_token_collapsed
cases.why: a >=12-char id/hash token collapses to '*' (session ids, request ids)

cases.name: mixed_case_lowered
cases.why: case is normalised (ToLowerInvariant / toLowerCase) so 'NULL' and 'null' key the same

cases.name: whitespace_collapsed_and_trimmed
cases.why: runs of whitespace (incl. tabs/newlines) collapse to one space and both ends are trimmed

cases.name: combined_all_transforms_in_order
cases.why: every transform together, exercising ORDER (lower -> iso -> query -> long-token -> whitespace -> trim)
